title: cyanus triumfettii subsp. lingulatus
intro: Cyanus triumfettii subsp. lingulatus, also known as Cornflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cornflower include soothing irritated eyes and skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting digestive health. Cornflower can be consumed as an herbal tea, used topically as an infusion, or taken as a supplement, allowing for various preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
